subHeader.title: Africapolis.org and the 2024 Update is produced by the Sahel and West Africa Club (SWAC) in collaboration with e-Geopolis.org.

list.item: Africapolis.org and the 2021 Update is produced by the Sahel and West Africa Club (SWAC) in collaboration with e-geopolis.org. This project is made possible by regular funding from SWAC members and by additional funding from USAID. The Africapolis project started in 2008 with support from the French Development Agency (AFD).

Africapolis contributes to the New Urban Agenda in Africa and the Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 11 to “make cities and human settlement inclusive, safe, resilient and sustainable” by providing policy makers and researchers with a unique insight into the African urban landscape and a strong basis for the analyses and strategies needed to support sustainable and co-ordinated urban development.

about.title: The Sahel and West Africa Club

list.item: SWAC is an independent, international platform. Its Secretariat is hosted at the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD). Its mission is to promote regional policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people in the Sahel and West Africa.

Agence Française de Développement (AFD), Austria, Belgium, Canada, CILSS, Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH, the ECOWAS Commission, the European Union, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, Spain, Switzerland, the UEMOA Commission and the United States.

list.title: Mapping a new urban reality.
list.subTitle: In 2015, more than 50% of Africans lived in urban agglomerations. Cities, urban planning and management are key to delivering on the SDGs and a New Urban Agenda. Africapolis data and evidence supports cities and governments to make urban areas more inclusive, productive and sustainable.
list.description: In 2015, more than 50% of Africans lived in urban agglomerations. Cities, urban planning and management are key to delivering on the SDGs and a New Urban Agenda. Africapolis data and evidence supports cities and governments to make urban areas more inclusive, productive and sustainable.

list.title: A new approach to defining urban.
list.subTitle: Starting from the ground up Africapolis adopts a systematic and consistent definition of urban. By combining demographic sources, satellite and aerial imagery, Africapolis enables comparative and long-term analyses of urban dynamics. It covers all 13,000 urban agglomerations with more than 10,000 inhabitants in 54 African countries.
list.description: Starting from the ground up Africapolis adopts a systematic and consistent definition of urban. By combining demographic sources, satellite and aerial imagery, Africapolis enables comparative and long-term analyses of urban dynamics. It covers all 13,000 urban agglomerations with more than 10,000 inhabitants in 54 African countries.

list.title: Understanding what drives urbanisation in Africa today.
list.subTitle: Africa has the fastest urban growth in the world. By 2050, Africa's urban areas will be home to an additional 950 million people. Capturing how this growth is changing the size, structure and functional features of urban networks is key for managing and planning future trends.
list.description: Africa has the fastest urban growth in the world. By 2050, Africa's urban areas will be home to an additional 950 million people. Capturing how this growth is changing the size, structure and functional features of urban networks is key for managing and planning future trends.

list.title: There are only few big cities but many small ones.
list.subTitle: In Africa, as elsewhere, there are only few big cities but many small ones. The continent has 11 urban areas with more than five million inhabitants and more than 7,500 secondary cities with fewer than 500,000 inhabitants. Yet, many of these urban agglomerations are absent from most international databases.
